commands.review_summary:
The PR changes two Gateway HTTP handlers to construct route URLs with a fixed `http://localhost` base instead of using the request `Host` header.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main routes both changed handlers through Host-derived URL bases, and a malformed Host value makes the URL constructor throw before normal route handling. I did not run live Gateway handler requests in this read-only review.

target.title: gateway: standardize URL base to localhost in mcp-http and tools-invoke handlers
target.body:
## Summary

Switches two remaining gateway HTTP handlers from a Host-header-derived URL base to the literal `"http://localhost"`, matching the pattern already used elsewhere in `src/gateway/` on `main`:

- `src/gateway/mcp-http.request.ts:78` — `validateMcpLoopbackRequest`
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.ts:30` — `handleToolsInvokeHttpRequest`

This closes the gap noted in #59696's review (`P3` finding pointing to `mcp-http.request.ts:78`) and adds the analogous fix in `tools-invoke-http.ts:30`, while staying on top of current `main`.

## Why this is a separate PR

`main` has independently landed the same `localhost`-base treatment for the other four handlers (`http-endpoint-helpers`, `models-http`, `session-kill-http`, `sessions-history-http`) since #59696 was opened, leaving these two as the remaining stragglers. Rebasing the original branch was not viable (`no merge base` vs current `main`), so a clean two-line PR off `main` HEAD is the lightest path forward. Suggesting #59696 can be closed in favor of this once merged.

## Real behavior proof

Repro with Node v22.22.1, comparing the two URL bases against several hostile and benign Host headers:

```
url                       | host             | old: host | pathname            | new: host | pathname
--------------------------+------------------+----------------------------------+--------------------------
/mcp                      | example.com      | example.com | /mcp               | localhost | /mcp
/mcp                      | evil.com:8080    | evil.com:8080 | /mcp             | localhost | /mcp
/mcp                      | [malformed       | THROW: ERR_INVALID_URL           | localhost | /mcp
/tools/invoke?x=1         | evil.com         | evil.com | /tools/invoke         | localhost | /tools/invoke
//evil.com/abs            | localhost        | evil.com | /abs                  | evil.com | /abs
```

**Observations:**

1. `url.pathname` (what these handlers actually consume) is identical before and after on every input → **no regression**.
2. `url.host` / `url.origin` becomes a trusted constant instead of attacker-controlled — any future code that compares these against an allowlist becomes deterministic.
3. Malformed Host headers no longer throw `ERR_INVALID_URL`, removing one trivial 400-amplification surface.

**What this fix does not address** (and is out of scope for the localhost-base change): schema-relative URLs like `//evil.com/abs` still resolve their authority from the request URL itself, not the base — visible in the last row. That's WHATWG URL spec behavior; if the gateway wants to defend against schema-relative authority injection it would need a separate input-side check on `req.url`.

## Security

No dependency, workflow, package-script, secret-handling, permission, or third-party execution surface is touched. Only the URL constructor's `base` argument changes value.

cc `@clawsweeper`
target.author: yonganzhang
